items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Is this all the manpower you can muster? It appears the Order truly is in decline. Or perhaps you think that little of me?

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Alessio, why bother? Look at yourself. You're a mess.

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Hmph, Luther, you self-righteous bastard. Should I be referring to you as Priest Luther? Or is it Primus Luther now?

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: No, of course not. You'd never dare claim the Primus seat yourself. Let me guess, you're propping up that foolish Echo-headed girl from the Ministry of Holy Rituals as a puppet? I hear she's even made it to the Privy Council.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: The Order stands without a Primus or Priest, and it will remain so for the foreseeable future, Alessio.

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: You're doing nothing, then? What of Barlin and Gnessa? They're allowing this nonsense to go unchecked?

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Acolytes Barlin and Gnessa have both sacrificed themselves. Martyred, along with the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Ah, there it is. You've lost your backbone and retreated back into your little shell again. Oh, Luther, are you planning to castrate the Order and hand it off to some noble family?

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Who's backing you this time? The grinning tigress of the Montellis? Or is it the Bane of Fisalia?

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Look around. Two prison escorts flank us. One for you, one for me. It's time we stand trial for our sins.

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Trial? Trial, you say?! Hahaha. No one holds the right to try me.

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: There's no Primus, no Priest, not even a representative from a family! Don't tell me you're planning to let the Ephor of Septimont decide the fate of a Ragunna Acolyte?

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Each and every citizen of Ragunna is qualified to judge you and me, Alessio.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: If the Primus indeed fell to the Threnodian's corruption, and lower-ranking Acolytes are proven to have been deceived, then as the Order's voice and ears, we must face the reckoning.

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Get up, Alessio. Ragunna is waiting.

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Luther... You can't be serious? Have you gone mad? You want us both to die? Together?!

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Without us, what will become of Ragunna? Let those savage families tear it apart? Become an annex of Septimont? And don't forget the predators lurking on the other side of the mist!

items.speaker: Alessio
items.text: Snap out of it, Luther. Ragunna won't survive without us! Centuries of work, the law and order we've built, our power and wealth. And you're ready to just hand it all away?!

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: ...You disgust me, Alessio. Since when did you start believing that Ragunna was ours to own?

items.speaker: Luther
items.text: Let's go. I've already made the arrangements. The people will count your crimes, then mine.
